data.title: Bitcoin rises to $92,000 as Congress seeks SEC approval for 401(k) investment. $77.3M outflows for Bitcoin ETFs, $42.4M for Ethereum ETFs.
data.description: Bitcoin has surged to $92,000 as there are discussions in Congress about the SEC potentially approving the inclusion of Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies in 401(k) retirement plans. Bitcoin ETFs experienced net outflows of $77.3 million on Thursday, while Ethereum ETFs saw net outflows of $42.4 million. The current cryptocurrency prices are as follows: Bitcoin (BTC) at $92,458.23, Ethereum (ETH) at $3,243.94, Solana (SOL) at $138.32, and XRP at $2.03. Market analyst Michael van de Poppe highlighted the continued high volatility of Bitcoin. For the full story, visit a financial news website.

data.title: BULGOLD finishes diamond drilling on Lutila Gold Project in Central Slovakia, spanning 32.2km² for gold deposits.
data.description: In Toronto on December 12, 2025, BULGOLD Inc. (TSXV: ZLTO) disclosed the completion of exploration diamond drilling on the Lutila Gold Project in Central Slovakia. The project spans 32.2km² and shows promise for quartz-adularia, low-sulfidation epithermal gold mineralisation.

data.title: New York requires AI transparency in ads as SAG-AFTRA advocates for No Fakes Act nationwide.
data.description: Legislation in New York state now requires disclosure of AI involvement in advertising campaigns. Additionally, the Screen Actors Guild - American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG-AFTRA) is pushing for the No Fakes Act to be implemented nationwide to protect against fraudulent practices. This development highlights the growing importance of transparency in the use of AI in advertising and the need for stronger regulations to combat fake content.

data.title: Jiuzi Holdings, Inc. expands private placement to $1 billion due to high demand from investors.
data.description: In Hangzhou, China on December 12, 2025, Jiuzi Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: JZXN) disclosed an expansion in its private placement financing, with a Memorandum of Understanding signed with institutional investors. The Company aims to raise a maximum of US$1 million through this agreement.

data.title: Google introduces Gemini Deep Research, AI tool enhancing research capabilities, competing with OpenAI's GPT-5.2.
data.description: Google, under Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG), has introduced an updated version of its research tool, Gemini Deep Research, aimed at transforming the utilization of AI in research and development. The enhanced Gemini Agent, based on the advanced Gemini 3 Pro model, was revealed on Thursday. Beyond generating research reports, the tool enables developers to incorporate Google's sophisticated SATA-model research functionalities into their own applications. Gemini Deep Research is capable of synthesizing extensive data and managing large information dumps efficiently. Google mentions that customers utilize this tool for various tasks. For more information, refer to the complete article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kimberly-Clark(NASDAQ: KMB) to announce Q4 & full year 2025 results on Jan 27, 2026, at 6:30 a.m. EST.
data.description: Kimberly-Clark (NASDAQ: KMB) has announced that it will release its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 on Tuesday, January 27, 2026. The report is scheduled to be released at around 6:30 a.m. EST, accompanied by a press release and additional materials. Following this release, Kimberly-Clark's management will conduct a hosting session to discuss the outcomes.

data.title: Holiday demand boosts interest in laser engraving tools; creators seek reliable systems to meet rising demand for customized gifts.
data.description: In New York on December 12, 2025, the holiday season has reignited interest in the technologies driving the current personalization trend. There is a growing demand for reliable CO2 laser cutter engravers and fiber laser engraving systems as creators strive to meet the increased demand for personalized gifts. This surge in interest has been noted by Monport, a company specializing in...

data.title: Humanity at a pivotal point in history per Beijing Review, signaling a new era in international relations.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, in Beijing, a significant moment in global history is unfolding, akin to what philosopher G.W.F. Hegel refers to as a nodal point. This moment heralds the beginning of a transformative period that will be chronicled in history books for years to come. A fresh paradigm in international relations is emerging, setting the stage for unprecedented shifts in the geopolitical landscape.

data.title: Delaware Court reverses $1 billion judgement against Byju Raveendran with fresh submissions made.
data.description: In a recent development on December 12, 2025, in Bengaluru, India, and New Delhi, the Delaware Court made a significant decision regarding Byju Raveendran, the Director of Byjus Investments Pvt. Ltd. The court reversed the previous $1 billion judgement against Raveendran. This decision came after Raveendran submitted new evidence through a motion to correct the initial judgement of $1 billion that was made on the 20th of...

data.title: Wall Street expects Lennar (NYSE: LEN) to report $2.18 per share in Q4 earnings (down from $4.03 last year) with $9.13 billion revenue. CEO retirement caused 0.7% share dip to $119.15.
data.description: Lennar Corporation (NYSE: LEN) is scheduled to announce its fourth-quarter earnings on Tuesday, Dec. 16, after market close. Analysts predict the Miami-based company will report earnings of $2.18 per share, a decrease from $4.03 per share in the same period last year. The consensus revenue estimate for the quarter is $9.13 billion, down from $9.95 billion in the previous year. Jonathan Jaffe, co-CEO and president of Lennar, recently announced his retirement on Nov. 14. Despite this news, Lennar's shares showed a decline of 0.7%, closing at $119.15 on Thursday. For more detailed information, including the latest analyst ratings, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dow Jones and Russell 2000 hit record highs, Nasdaq recovers losses, Broadcom expects AI chip sales to double.
data.description: Both the Dow Jones and Russell 2000 indices reached historic peaks, with the Nasdaq recovering from initial dips. Broadcom anticipates a 100% increase in sales of AI chips. Dow Jones futures are up, but technology stocks are experiencing a decline, leading to Broadcom being the most recent AI industry leader to face a sell-off post-earnings report.
